title: Warning when you're about to change multiple pulses
excerpt: I saw that someone has requested this in the past, but it would be extremely helpful if there could be a warning when you’re about to change multiple pulses. We’ve had a few instances of someone accidentally selecting ev…

posts.author: bpdesign (Beth Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml: I saw that someone has requested this in the past, but it would be extremely helpful if there could be a warning when you’re about to change multiple pulses. We’ve had a few instances of someone accidentally selecting everything on a board (with the “select all” shortcut), making a change and changing everything on the board. The short “undo” option at the top isn’t enough – you’re conditioned to ignore that because it comes up so often when you do anything. And usually the undo option is gone by the time you realize you’ve changed multiple items.

posts.author: MichaelSA (Michael Schweizer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please do something about this problem. I recently clicked on a button that sends out our promo material and a hundred items were activated because I accidentally hit comm-a when I thought I was in a different application. So result: 190 people got wrong and outdated mails and I lost a big amount of data because did not have the time to change the over 300 items back and this also in a timeframe of 48 hours. This is a major fail. A simple Warning when entering a field and multiple items are selected would do. Please help us out here. Vote and Write to Monday about this.
Thanks, Michael
